figures.caption: Figure 1: A case illustrates how subject confounders introduce spurious correlations in personality understanding. Subjects 1 and 2 (Training) associate white female appearances and gentle tones with high agreeableness, misleading the model to infer Subject 3 (white female with low agreeableness).
Table 1: Comprehensive Performance Comparison of Models on CFI-V2 and DMSP datasets. Best results are bolded.
Table 3: Ablation study of key components on the DMSP dataset. Best results are bolded.
Table 2: Ablation study of key components on the CFV-V2 dataset. Best results are bolded.

Figure 3: The architecture of our DCAN. Multimodal inputs 𝑋 are encoded and projected into a shared latent space X𝑚 . And then BACL eliminates observable confounders (e.g., demographics) via back-door adjustment F𝑚 with statistical prototype confounder dictionaries D𝑚 𝑏𝑎𝑐𝑘 . FACL addresses unobservable confounders via front-door adjustment U𝑚 by learning mediator D𝑚 𝑚𝑒𝑑 and global confounder dictionaries D𝑚 𝑔𝑙𝑜𝑏𝑎𝑙 . Finally, the deconfounded multimodal representations U𝑚 are fused by a cross-modal transformer for personality understanding.
Table 4: Comprehensive fairness comparison on CFI-V2 and DMSP datasets. ↓ indicates lower is better.
Figure 5: Out-of-distribution (OOD) evaluation on CFI-V2 across demographic attributes.
Figure 4: Illustration of the proposed causal intervention pipeline: (a) BACL targets observable confounders (e.g., demographics); (b) FACL targets unobservable confounders (e.g., subject emotions).
